item.title: I’m still calling it a Bug out bag
item.description:
https://www.npr.org/2025/05/07/nx-s1-5320173/budget-emergency-go-bag
Award Edit: Thanks for the award, kind stranger! A gentle reminder that the article is about how to make a cheap emergency bag, it focuses on socio-economically disadvantaged people, it does have some really good suggestions. I sent it to a few people in my phone and the responses range from “fuck off, but thanks” to “who are you, Sterling Archer?” Second edit: There are so many great comments suggesting things I never even thought about, you guys are the best, thank you!! This list is not comprehensive and nor is it a literal checklist that needs to be 100%. I should have stressed this more in my original post. This bag is an EXTREMELY personal item. Only you or your family should be aware of the location and contents of this bag. Your bug out bag should be grabbed reflexively, the day of crisis is not the time to rummage through your bag and curse your former self. Train yourself like we do for wallet, keys, phone sequence Most viral edit: Thanks for the upvotes, my dudes. And thanks for all the suggested items, I expanded the list based on your comments. Credit to: @Exyr @PocketCleric @Zortroz @XaoJet In case you need a list version of the contents [ ] A good sturdy bag (duh) [ ] Gallon of water (3 days worth) [ ] Long shelf life food [ ] Prescriptions [ ] Basic first aid kit including bug repellant [ ] Important docs in a waterproof or ziploc bag (passport, Social security card etc) [ ] Emergency repair supplies like work gloves, rope, duct tape, sewing kit, batteries, flashlights, lighters, pocket knife etc [ ] Personal hygiene items and a towel [ ] Power bank (s), flash drives, solar chargers [ ] Cash [ ] Personal Protection item (firearm, zapper, spicy nope spray etc) [ ] Feminine Hygiene Products [ ] Water filters and salt [ ] Life straws For reasons that don’t need to be public, I have always had a bug out bag ready to go. It has my docs, cash, one credit card, a couple of t shirts, underwear etc. This article was a good reminder to go through it and make sure it’s well stocked. I shouldn’t have to say this but just in case, this isn’t Bourne Identity, you’re not going to be using your bug out bag to escape authorities in a thrilling way. If you have to use this emergency bag, it is going to be during a crisis in your life. I had to grab my bug out bag once, and I was super glad for the past me that stocked it with the right things. I’d rather the event that forced me to grab that bag never take place again but that’s not in my control. “Better to have one than to need one” - Roger Smith
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: Looks like he took the easy route, he is getting a luxury jet 'palace in the sky' as a gift
item.description: Qatar to Gift $400 Million Plane to President Trump: - According to ABC News, the Trump administration is set to accept a luxury Boeing 747-8 jumbo jet—valued at around $400 million—from the Qatari royal family. - The plane will serve as Air Force One for Trump until just before he leaves office, after which ownership will transfer to his presidential library foundation. - Sources say this may be the most valuable gift ever given to the U.S. by a foreign government. - Attorney General Pam Bondi and Trump’s White House lawyer David Warrington concluded the arrangement is legally permissible. - Bondi—who herself was a registered foreign agent for Qatar—provided the legal memo approving the deal. Do you know who investigates corruption? DOJ. Currently DoJ is headed by Pam Bondi. Prior to her current job, Aa FARA-registered lobbyist, Bondi lobbied Congress on behalf of Qatar, earning. $115,000 per month. Nothing to see here - The DOJ and White House determined the gift does not constitute bribery, since it isn’t tied to any official act. - Bondi also argued it doesn’t violate the Constitution’s ban on foreign gifts because it’s being given to the U.S. Air Force and, later, a nonprofit. - Neither the White House, DOJ, nor the Qatari embassy responded to ABC’s requests for comment. - Experts note the aircraft’s $400 million price tag doesn’t include the additional security and communications upgrades needed for presidential transport. - Remember the post 9/11 war on terror where trillions were spent invading Afghanistan and Iraq, the attack came from Saudi and Qatari native Feb. 7, 2003 -- American officials say the FBI and CIA just missed capturing the al Qaeda leader believed to have organized the 9/11 attacks - and now believed to be planning a new attack against Americans - because it appears he was protected and tipped off by a member of the royal family in Qatar, Abdullah Bin Khalid al-Thani.

item.title: I'm not going to say I'm a true believer, but I think it's on to something
item.description: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dead_Internet_theory TL;DR follows: Many years ago, I was channel-surfing and came upon a reality show whose premise was people with bizarre addictions, or something. The segment featured a man who was supposedly in love with his car, sexually. Lots of heartfelt narration, and clips to camera of him "making out" with his car. It was, to put it mildly, unconvincing, and I saw the whole of "unscripted television" tip its hand in that moment — the carfucker wasn't real, the premise of the show was a sham, and none of this actually existed outside the purview of some writers' room somewhere. The world, in that moment, became so much easier to understand. More recently, I was randomly surfing and came upon an online discussion of a recent "Black Mirror" episode, and the comments section was stuffed with people arguing about the plot points in the episode as though they were real events that had actually happened, and not as a ham-fisted technology metaphor for exploring various facets of the human condition. And it was at that point at it seemed that the whole of the "social content" space tipped its hand — I knew I was watching LLMs pretend to argue with other LLMs about a work of fiction, in some bizarre ouroboros of make-believe, all ostensibly for the purpose increasing "engagement", and making it look like there was peer consensus around this kind of thing. And now I wonder how much of this place is just bots propping up the carcass. I have noticed a theme of "all the real users left a while ago" in some comments, maybe the LLMs are even saying the quiet part out loud?
item.dc:creator: 0xDEC0DE

item.dc:creator: TheUnnamedPoet

item.title: THE DAM ON THE WRONG SIDE
item.description: THE DAM ON THE WRONG SIDE (A Lullaby for the Liars) Every stone, every brick, every whisper laid thick— a wall of pretend, a truthless parade, stacked high with the lies they made. They built and built, with sharpened pride, but they were building the dam on the wrong side. Yes— they were building the dam on the wrong side. Some say, “Repeat a lie, and people will believe it.” But I heard the truth in the cracks beneath it. I saw too much. Heard what they couldn’t name— noises that came from thoughts, from dusk, from the double-headed eagle’s flame. It came in music. In poems left behind. In empty libraries, where silence had a spine. And me— I was hanging from the chandelier, wishing to curl up inside a flower, drink some dew, sleep through their power. But I found strength. And I came back. I ate every book, tore through every track— looking for answers, only to learn the answers had always burned their way into me. There are no lies in the universe. Only echoes. Only seeing. And the more they tried to hide, the more we started feeling. We know where your cemeteries lie. We know how you chained the light. You tarnished innocence with your grief, but angels don’t die. You thought you were clever— stacking the bodies, sealing the flood, sacrificing purity to keep your hands clean of blood. But you were building the dam on the wrong side. Yes— you were building the dam on the wrong side. And now the river comes, not with war, but with memory. With music you can’t silence. With eyes you can’t meet. With children humming tunes from places you dared mistreat. The flood doesn’t scream. It sees. It knows. It presses. And now, you feel it— not crashing, but confessing. Because the dam is cracking, not from the front, but from inside. You were never holding back the water. You were just the ones who couldn’t see the tide. You built it up with all your might. With fear, and steel, and genocidal pride. But you were building the dam on the wrong side. Again, and again— on the wrong side. Poem by Vesp - Artwork by Litvinova Julia fallen angels with bound wings 2016
item.dc:creator: Multipass

item.title: The best little helpers
item.description: These disposable litters boxes are the best, and I’m putting together a bunch now so it’s easier to swap them out. The kittens are *almost* exclusively using the litter boxes so the time is coming when I’ll be able to let them out into the kitten room!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays
